_note: Source of truth for site copy, seeded from the Vinea Obsidian vault. Stats carry sources. The Vault-Researcher agent may extend/refine this.

brand.name: Vinea
brand.tagline: Autonomous harvesting for the greenhouses you already have.
brand.oneLiner: A modular autonomous robot that harvests and scouts inside existing Dutch commercial greenhouses — no infrastructure rebuild — sold as a monthly service that gets harder to leave every season it runs.
brand.source: Idea Anatomy — Full Compilation: One-Line Pitch

hero.heading: Autonomous harvesting for the greenhouses you already have.
hero.body: Vinea is a modular robot that harvests and scouts inside existing commercial greenhouses — no rebuild, no capex. Deployed as a monthly service that costs less than the labour it replaces from month one.

problem.heading: Greenhouses are running out of hands.
problem.body: Dutch tomato, cucumber, and pepper operators spend roughly €1.6M a year per 5-hectare site on manual harvesting and scouting — at the exact moment seasonal labour is structurally disappearing. The only funded robot on the market requires rebuilding the greenhouse, leaving ~95% of operators with no option that fits their existing infrastructure.
bullets: ~8 workers per hectare, ~€38,200 per worker per year
Reports of up to 75% less available greenhouse labour
No automation that works in existing greenhouse infrastructure

stats.label: annual harvest + scouting labour per 5-ha site
of the market unserved by rebuild-required robots
reported drop in available greenhouse labour

product.heading: One base. Swappable modules. Works in your rows today.
product.body: A single configurable robot base accepts interchangeable modules. Same chassis, compute, and navigation across every customer — only the gripper, row-width adapter, and licensed modules change. It scales like a product line, not bespoke builds.

modules.name: Harvest
modules.desc: Autonomous picking and de-leafing. Computer-vision-guided, with crop-specific soft grippers — tomato first.

modules.name: Scout
modules.desc: Daily inspection passes that flag disease and pests 3–5 days before visible symptoms, with grower-dashboard alerts.

product.source: Pillar 5 — Solution + MVP Hardware Concept v1

capabilities.heading: Off-the-shelf vision, purpose-built where it counts.
bullets: 6-DOF arm with a soft, adaptive gripper that warps to fruit shape to prevent bruising
Coarse-to-fine vision: a plant-wide camera locates fruit, an eye-in-hand camera verifies ripeness before picking
Runs inside existing greenhouse row infrastructure — no rebuild required
Every deployment improves the shared model: the product gets better each season
capabilities.source: MVP Hardware Concept v1 + Pillar 8 — Moat

raas.heading: Robotics as a service. Priced against a wage.
raas.body: No upfront purchase. Hardware, software, maintenance, and data in one monthly subscription with a 12-month minimum. It turns a multi-hundred-thousand-euro capex decision into an opex line directly comparable to a worker's wage.

stats.label: net monthly saving per robot vs. the labour it replaces

market.heading: 2026 is the dawn of horticultural robotics.
market.body: Collapsing seasonal labour, a 70%+ fall in vision-sensor costs since 2020, a €10M raise by the first funded competitor, and hundreds of millions in Dutch agricultural-innovation funding have converged. The technology that wasn't buildable by a small team in 2020 is buildable in 2026.

stats.label: serviceable Dutch market at full penetration (~587 farms)

roadmap.heading: Prove the pick, then earn the channel.

milestones.title: Validation
milestones.desc: Talk to real Dutch growers; confirm the pain before building.

milestones.title: Prototype the core bet
milestones.desc: Reliable, bruise-free autonomous tomato picking at commercial throughput — the one number everything depends on.

milestones.title: Direct pilot
milestones.desc: First reference deployment in Westland / Lansingerland.

milestones.title: Channel + expand
milestones.desc: Partner with established horticulture suppliers; expand toward Belgium and Spain.

roadmap.source: Compilation: GTM sequencing + Locked vs Open

about.heading: Founder-led, obsession over credentials.
about.body: Vinea is being built by Krishaanth Ramaraj, from an EE/hardware foundation and a decade-long pull toward agricultural robotics. The plan is honest: build a small cross-faculty team to prove something real, then bring in deeper greenhouse and robotics expertise. Cofounder search is active — the filter is obsession, not titles.

contact.heading: See it pick.
contact.body: If you run a greenhouse, want to partner, or want to build this — reach out.
